thread.title: 'It sucks to see': Eagles' Saquon Barkley rips NY Giants over Daniel Jones release
thread.title_full: 'It sucks to see': Eagles' Saquon Barkley rips NY Giants over Daniel Jones release

author: , Courier News
title: 'It sucks to see': Eagles' Saquon Barkley rips NY Giants over Daniel Jones release
text:
'It sucks to see': Eagles' Saquon Barkley rips NY Giants over Daniel Jones release
PHILADELPHIA − [Eagles](https://sportsdata.usatoday.com/football/nfl/teams/philadelphia-eagles/354) running back Saquon Barkley spent the previous five seasons with Daniel Jones and the [New York Giants](https://sportsdata.usatoday.com/football/nfl/teams/new-york-giants/351), becoming close friends with the quarterback.
So Barkley didn't mince words when he heard that the Giants decided to bench Jones earlier this week, then release him Friday upon Jones' request, saying: "It sucks to see how everything went down for him over there."
The Giants demoted Jones to third string, behind Tommy DeVito and Drew Lock coming off their bye week Monday. Jones practiced as a safety on the scout team Thursday. [He asked for and was granted his release Friday.](https://www.northjersey.com/story/sports/nfl/giants/2024/11/22/daniel-jones-released-by-new-york-giants-after-six-nfl-seasons/76497767007/)
Barkley, who left the Giants as a free agent last spring after six seasons in New York, said he has kept in touch with Jones throughout this season.
"Our friendship has stayed close throughout the whole process of me being here," Barkley said. "It sucks to see how everything went down for him over there. I have nothing but great things to say about him. You’re not going to really find anybody who can say a lot of negative things about him.
"But it’s the NFL. Hopefully, wherever he ends up next, they’re going to get a guy who can come in and work."
Then Barkley strongly suggested that Jones will be better off finding a new team by taking another shot at the Giants, who are 2-8, in last place in the NFC East.
Barkley has 1,137 yards rushing through 10 games, second in the NFL to Baltimore's Derrick Henry, who has 1,185 yards in 11 games.
"It didn’t work for me over there, and I’m doing well over here," Barkley said. "Hopefully, he can find the same kind of fresh start and success."
Contact Martin Frank at mfrank@delawareonline.com. Follow on X @Mfranknfl.
